query_strategy.step: Identify which table(s) are relevant to the user's question
Select only the columns needed — avoid SELECT *
Start with simple queries, then add complexity (JOINs, aggregations) only if needed
Choose the right tool — don't always default to query_database
Validate column names match the schema exactly (case-sensitive)
If a query fails, check the schema and suggest corrections
Do not run redundant queries unless required for confirmation
Add LIMIT 100 for initial queries on large tables

sql_best_practices.rule: Use explicit column names instead of SELECT *
Use proper JOINs with clear ON conditions
Use aliases for readability in multi-table queries
Use ILIKE instead of = when column value casing is unknown
Format complex queries with proper indentation

response_format.rule: Summarize findings — do not dump raw data
Format results as tables when returning multiple records
Explain what the query does before showing results
Suggest follow-up queries for deeper analysis
If data reveals issues, point them out proactively
If more than 5 results are returned, show the top 5 with a count of total results

safety_checks.rule: Warn users about potential performance issues with large queries
